commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=b8d598bfec06f8a780ab90821aacbc6bf2aedaaa)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the OpenAI embedding provider to preserve an `openai/` model prefix for non-native base URLs and adds unit tests for native and non-native request bodies.

PR surface: Source +12, Tests +63. Total +75 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the OpenClaw-side mismatch: current source shows `openai/` is stripped before request body and provider identity. I did not run a live Requesty call, so the external 400 remains supported by the linked issue rather than reproduced here.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor follow-up for the trimming regression and real behavior proof; automation cannot supply the contributor's non-native router proof.

target.title: fix(openai-embedding): preserve openai/ prefix for non-native base URLs
target.body:
## Summary

Preserve `openai/` model prefix for non-native OpenAI embedding base URLs.

When `agents.defaults.memorySearch.provider` is `openai` but `remote.baseUrl`
points to a non-native OpenAI router (e.g. Requesty), OpenClaw was stripping
the `openai/` prefix from the embedding model name, breaking memory search in
two ways:

1. Non-native routers reject the request because they expect the
   provider-qualified model name (`openai/text-embedding-3-small`).
2. Existing memory index identity no longer matches (`openai/text-embedding-3-small`
   vs. `text-embedding-3-small`), forcing an index rebuild.

## Root cause

`normalizeOpenAiModel()` in `extensions/openai/embedding-provider.ts`
unconditionally strips the `openai/` prefix. The resolved base URL determines
whether the prefix should be preserved, but the normalizer has no access to it.

## Fix

- Add `isNativeOpenAiBaseUrl()` helper to check whether the resolved base URL
  points to the native OpenAI API.
- After resolving the embedding client, restore the `openai/` prefix on the
  model when the base URL is non-native and the original model had the prefix.
- Native `api.openai.com` behavior is unchanged.

## Tests and validation

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/openai/embedding-provider.test.ts
# 9 passed (3 new: native URL strips prefix, non-native preserves prefix,
#            non-native prefix in request body)

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/embeddings-model-normalize.test.ts
# 3 passed (existing prefix stripping behavior)
```

## Real behavior proof

The fix is a pure classifier change entirely contained in the OpenAI embedding
provider plugin. Tests exercise the exact condition (native vs. non-native base
URL) at both the provider model identity and the HTTP request body layer.

Live provider proof was not run — the change is deterministic and covered by
the unit tests.

## Verification

- `pnpm test:changed` — only embedding-provider files changed
- Unit tests confirm:
  - Native OpenAI base URL → `openai/` prefix stripped (unchanged)
  - Non-native base URL + `openai/` prefix → prefix preserved in `provider.model`
  - Non-native base URL + `openai/` prefix → prefix preserved in HTTP request body

## Risk

None. Native OpenAI API path is fully unchanged. Non-native routers gain the
correct behavior (preserving `openai/` prefix). Tests cover positive
(prefix preserved) and negative (prefix stripped when native) cases.

Fixes #92124
target.author: xialonglee
